text:

author: polariscampus
text:
Kids These Days Episode 11

A college exam made Anant write code on paper.

So he built a platform where colleges can finally test code by actually running it.

No handwriting. No guessing. Just real code.

author: thepolarisfellowship
text:
@fam.india joins The Polaris Fellowship as an Ecosystem Partner.🤝🏻

Trusted by over 10 million users and backed by some of the world’s leading investors including Google, Elevation Capital, and Accel, Fam has become one of India’s fastest-growing consumer fintech companies in recent years.

From launching India’s first numberless card to products like prepaid cards, UPI payments, and more specially for Gen Z, they’re upping the bar on how young India interacts with money.

We’re excited to welcome Fam to the growing Polaris Fellowship ecosystem, alongside founders, operators, investors, and some of the country’s most ambitious technology companies.

#ThePolarisFellowship #Ai #Tech #Startup

text:

We asked students what they would do with it.

Comment Scholarship to get all the details.

author: thepolarisfellowship
text:
@sarvam.ai_official joins The Polaris Fellowship as a Productivity Partner. 🤝🏻

Headquartered in Bengaluru, Sarvam AI is one of India’s leading frontier AI companies, holding a notable estimated 30% share of India’s enterprise-grade Indic Large Language Model (LLM) market, and is valued at $1.5 billion.

In other words, they’re rapidly switching up the AI game in the country, with world-class research, multilingual models, and technology designed for India’s scale.

We’re excited to welcome Sarvam AI to the growing Polaris Fellowship ecosystem, alongside founders, operators, investors, and some of the country’s most ambitious technology companies.

#ThePolarisFellowship #Ai #startup #technology #IndianStartups

author: thepolarisfellowship
text:
10,000+ applications? You kept us rolling, and honestly, we’re floored!We’re genuinely humbled by the response. ❤️

We’re going through every single one, personally, line by line. No skimming, no shortcuts, promise.

So keep those 📩 open, updates coming your way soon.

This is just the start. Follow @thepolarisfellowship for India’s biggest student fellowship 🔥

[Polaris School of Technology, The Polaris Fellowship, technology, coding, Ai, entrepreneurship, startup, tech enthusiasts]

text:
Kids These Days- Episode 10

Buying clothes online shouldn’t feel like a gamble.

author: polariscampus
text:
College fees should never decide your future.

If you’re an engineering student, this scholarship could cover up to ₹2 lakh of your education.

Applications close soon. Comment SCHOLARSHIP and we’ll send you the details.

author: polariscampus
text: From taking a drop year and facing doubts from people around him to earning a prestigious LFX Mentorship with a $3,000 stipend, Divakar Sharma’s journey is proof that consistency beats criticism. His story shows how the right mentors, supportive friends, and the courage to keep trying can completely change your future. If you’re a student wondering whether it’s worth starting, this is your sign to take that first step.

author: engineeringdigest.in
text: Video by Vipul Tyagi on June 27, 2026. May be a selfie of one or more people, beard, eyeglasses, screen and text that says '2026 Btech'.

author: polariscampus
text: We’ve launched the Polaris All India Scholarship. Here’s the complete breakdown.

author: polariscampus
text:
Wherever you’re starting your B.Tech in 2026, we will BACK YOU! 🌠

Introducing the All India Polaris Scholarship.

Ten students, selected from across the country, backed at the very beginning of their BTech journey with something real.

Rank 1 to 3 win ₹2,00,000 each - cash they can use however their journey demands. Fees, tools, courses, breathing room. You name it.

Rank 4 to 10 win a MacBook each - the most powerful laptop in the world, theirs from day one of engineering college.

Here’s how it works:
 → Register for the Polaris Aptitude Test before 15th July
 → Top 500 write a scholarship essay
 → Top 50 move to a video interview
 → 10 winners. 3 get ₹2 lakh. 7 get a MacBook.

If you’re starting BTech in 2026 and are serious about building a future for yourself, registration link for the Polaris Aptitude Test (PAT) is in the comments and in our bio. Apply now!

🧷Link in BIO & COMMENTS! 

#AllIndiaPolaris Scholarship #Polaris #EngineeringIndia #BTech2026 #Scholarship

Link in Bio.

author: himeeshmadaan
text:
In today’s AI era, the biggest fear is not just losing jobs… it’s losing direction.

Mukul Rustagi opens up about a world where degrees are no longer enough, tech jobs are changing fast, and an entire generation is confused about what to learn, which career to choose, and where money will be made in the next 5–10 years.

In this powerful podcast, he shares:

How AI is changing the future of tech jobs and why being “cheap talent” is no longer enough
Why YouTube and ChatGPT can become the two biggest tools for young people to build their future
How students can use skills, proof of work, and global opportunities to reach their first ₹1 crore
Why the next wave of wealth may come from solopreneurs, AI ventures, and product builders
The mistakes young entrepreneurs make while building ideas, companies, and wealth

This isn’t just a conversation about AI or technology.

It’s a roadmap for every young person who feels confused about career, money, skills, and the future.

Watch the full conve

author: polariscampus
text:
Kids These Days- Episode 9

Every home has asked the same question:

“Aaj kya banayein?”

So Ishaan and Manas built an AI that looks inside your fridge and tells you exactly what to cook.

author: polariscampus
text:
Kids These Days- Episode 8

Most people ignore scam texts.

Soham built an AI that reads them for you, scores the risk, and explains exactly why they’re dangerous.

author: polariscampus
text:
What impressed Aayush Gupta, a founding team member at FAM, wasn’t just the ideas it was the questions.

From startup validation to RBI compliance, Polaris students were thinking beyond products and exploring how businesses are actually built.

His biggest takeaway? Curiosity is one of the most valuable skills a builder can develop.

Because in a world where AI can execute, asking better questions becomes the real advantage.

author: polariscampus
text:
Most legal AI tools help lawyers research.

This one goes a step further.

Built by a Polaris student, LexForge helps lawyers build arguments, challenge them through a devil’s advocate mode, and even simulate courtroom debates.

The goal isn’t to replace lawyers, it’s to help them prepare better before stepping into court.

A fascinating example of AI solving real-world problems with privacy and trust at its core.

author: polariscampus
text:
Kids These Days — Episode 7

Most students use AI to answer questions.

Rehan built an AI that studies with you.

Upload your notes and it turns them into summaries, quizzes, flashcards, and even podcasts.

author: polariscampus
text:
The best way to evaluate a college isn’t through brochures or rankings.

It’s by visiting the campus, talking to the students, meeting the mentors, and seeing how learning actually happens.

Sometimes, that’s all the clarity you need.

author: polariscampus
text:
Most students wait until their final year to start building a career. Polaris students are doing it in Year 1.

₹1 Cr+ in stipends, 109 opportunities, 35 global open-source selections, and internships across 11 countries.

This isn’t a placement report. It’s what happens when you focus on proof of work from Day 1.

author: polariscampus
text:
What is the strongest tangible proof of coding competence in real-world scenarios?

Open Source it is. Which is strongly engraved into the culture of Polaris.

This year, 18 Polaris students became part of that culture through Google Summer of Code 2026.

And they’re not just contributing locally. They’re now working across open-source ecosystems spanning 9 countries, contributing to real-world systems in:
- cybersecurity
- healthcare & genomics
- developer infrastructure
- ⁠geospatial systems
- ⁠programming languages

Working alongside experienced mentors, building systems used by developers and organizations across the world.

But behind every selection is a completely different story.
While some students started with utmost clarity, some rebuilt from scratch after setbacks. Some learned by breaking things, fixing them, and trying again.
What connects all of them is simple:
They showed up consistently.
 They kept building.

And they contributed before they felt “ready.” That’s what o
